personality_one_liner: Un centro di comando atletico e resistente, progettato per scansionare rapidamente i dati durante l'allenamento con un'estetica scura, calma ed energica.

spacing.rule: Generous intentional padding around content. Avoid empty halves of the screen by using single-column width minus padding. Touch targets >= 44x44pt.

dont's: No glassmorphism on dense data screens. Use solid tonal backgrounds (surfaceSecondary) for durability and clarity.

screens.name: Schede di Allenamento
screens.purpose: Home screen to select and start workout routines.

states.empty: Illustration/image of kettlebells, 'Nessuna scheda creata' and a primary CTA 'Crea Nuova Scheda'.
states.error: Message 'Errore nel caricamento delle schede' with a 'Riprova' button.

screens.layout: Large header 'Le Mie Schede'. Single column list of workout cards using surfaceSecondary with 1px border. Each card shows title, muscle groups, and estimated time. Sticky primary CTA 'Crea Nuova Scheda' pinned at bottom above tab bar.

screens.name: Libreria Esercizi
screens.purpose: Browse and search the exercise database in Italian.
screens.layout: Sticky search bar at top. Horizontal scrollable segmented controls for muscle groups (Petto, Dorso, Gambe, etc.). List of exercises with 56pt height rows, leading thumbnail image (using surfaceTertiary if no image), Title, and Subtitle (Equipment).

screens.name: Sessione di Allenamento Attiva
screens.purpose: Track live workout progress, log sets/reps/weight, and manage rest periods.

states.error: Error dialog to save progress locally.

screens.layout: Top bar: 'Termina' button + current elapsed time. Body: Stacked modules of exercises. Each module contains rows for Set #, Reps input, Weight input, and a large checkbox to mark done. Rest Timer Overlay: Fixed panel at the bottom of the screen displaying a large countdown timer in Barlow Condensed, with Start/Skip controls. It uses solid surfaceSecondary background with top borderStrong for high contrast.

screens.name: Progressi
screens.purpose: Visualize training volume, 1RM estimates, and workout frequency.

states.empty: Message 'Completa un allenamento per vedere i tuoi progressi'.

screens.layout: High-contrast data visualization. Slim weekly line/bar chart for completed sessions. Compact stat row above chart (Allenamenti, Volume Totale). Highlight current value, use brandPrimary for chart accents. Neutral background.

screens.name: Storico Allenamenti
screens.purpose: Log of past completed workout sessions.
screens.layout: Vertical timeline list showing Date, Workout Name, and total Volume lifted. Subtle dividers between rows. Clean typography with monospace/condensed font for metrics.

screens.name: Dettaglio Esercizio
screens.purpose: Show exercise instructions, target muscles, and personal history.
screens.layout: Hero image/GIF at top with gradient-to-dark scrim. Exercise title and description text in Italian. 'Storico Performance' section at the bottom showing past weights lifted.

empty_schede.alt: Kettlebells and weights in a modern dark gym

instructions_to_main_agent: Design specifically for iOS. Ensure safe area views are respected so content doesn't sit under the notch/dynamic island.
The app must be entirely localized in Italian.
Implement 'Barlow Condensed' exclusively for metrics, timers, and large titles to create an athletic, live-data feel. Use 'Satoshi' for body text.
The Active Workout screen is the core feature. Ensure inputs for reps/weight are large (min 44x44pt) to allow easy interaction with sweaty fingers. Disable autocorrect and use numeric keyboards.

The Rest Timer in the Active Workout must be a fixed overlay at the bottom of the scrolling content. It should use a solid background (no blur, per Dark-First Utility rules) for durability and fast scanning.
Always use a gradient-to-dark scrim (top to bottom, transparent to >=70% surfaceInverse) between images and overlaid text.
Keep interactions instant and reactive. Avoid slow cinematic animations; the app should feel machine-fast.
Use solid tonal backgrounds (surfaceSecondary, surfaceTertiary) and spacing instead of glassmorphism or shadows.
Apply haptic feedback to marking sets complete, starting timers, and finishing rests.
